task_completion.description: Verify the 'Annual Leave' policy is deactivated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Sentrifugo Leave Types configuration screen showing 'Annual Leave' with an inactive, disabled, or deleted status.

task_completion.description: Verify a company-wide announcement titled 'Plant Operations Pause' is posted.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Sentrifugo web interface rather than direct database manipulation or terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sentrifugo web application open in a web browser, not terminal windows executing SQL commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent navigated through the Sentrifugo menus to perform the actions.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of intermediate Sentrifugo screens (e.g., HR > Configuration, Leave > Leave Types, or HR > Announcements) indicating genuine navigation.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just write the expected outcomes in a text file to spoof completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The changes (status, leave types, announcements) must be visible within the actual Sentrifugo UI, not typed out in a text editor or terminal output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected employment status to create is 'Furloughed - Unpaid'. The leave type to deactivate is 'Annual Leave'. The announcement title must be 'Plant Operations Pause'. CRITICAL: The directive document lists the target employees as 'Production staff' named Michael Taylor, James Thomas, William White, and Ethan Martin. However, in the actual Sentrifugo database, the corresponding employee IDs are assigned to completely different names and departments: EMP008 (Ashley Johnson, HR), EMP011 (Matthew Garcia, Marketing), EMP014 (Stephanie Brown, Customer Success), and EMP017 (Brian Taylor, Finance). The agent must update the status for these specific IDs (EMP008, EMP011, EMP014, EMP017) or their corresponding actual names in the database, regardless of the contradiction in the directive text.
